title: Numbers in Dart
content:
### Numeric Types

Dart provides two numeric types: `int` and `double`. They both extend the `num` type.

| Type | Size | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `int` | Platform-dependent | Integer values up to 2^63 on 64-bit |
| `double` | 64-bit | Double-precision floating point (IEEE 754) |
| `num` | - | Supertype of int and double |

### Integer Operations

```dart
void main() {
  int a = 10;
  int b = 3;

  print(a + b);   // 13
  print(a - b);   // 7
  print(a * b);   // 30
  print(a ~/ b);  // 3 (integer division)
  print(a % b);   // 1 (modulus)
  print(-a);      // -10 (unary minus)
}
```

### Double Operations

```dart
void main() {
  double x = 10.5;
  double y = 3.2;

  print(x + y);  // 13.7
  print(x - y);  // 7.3
  print(x * y);  // 33.6
  print(x / y);  // 3.28125
}
```

### Useful Properties and Methods

```dart
void main() {
  int value = -42;
  double pi = 3.14159;

  print(value.abs());      // 42
  print(value.isNegative); // true
  print(value.isEven);     // true
  print(value.isOdd);      // false

  print(pi.round());       // 3
  print(pi.floor());       // 3
  print(pi.ceil());        // 4
  print(pi.toStringAsFixed(2)); // '3.14'
}
```

### Numeric Constants

```dart
void main() {
  print(double.infinity);  // Infinity
  print(double.nan);       // NaN
  print(double.maxFinite); // 1.7976931348623157e+308
}
```

### Converting Between int and double

```dart
void main() {
  int a = 10;
  double aDouble = a.toDouble(); // 10.0

  double b = 3.7;
  int bInt = b.toInt();          // 3 (truncates)
  int bRound = b.round();        // 4
}
```

> **Key Point:** Integer division uses `~/` and returns an `int`. Regular division `/` always returns a `double`.

quiz.question: What does the ~/ operator do in Dart?
